title: homeMaker-completion
goal: Ship the remaining 4 milestones to make homeMaker fully branded, all modules functional end-to-end, and Home Assistant integration bidirectional.

milestones.title: M1: Complete Rebrand
milestones.description: Update all UI strings referencing 'protoLabs Studio' to 'homeMaker'. Update bug report links from protoMaker to homeMaker repo.

phases.name: update-all-protolabs-studio-ui-references-to-homemaker
phases.title: Update all protoLabs Studio UI references to homeMaker
phases.description: Replace all user-facing 'protoLabs Studio' and 'protoLabs Logo' strings with 'homeMaker' branding in setup-view, welcome-step, dashboard-view, welcome-view, sidebar, dialogs, and settings. Update bug report links to homeMaker repo.
acceptanceCriteria: No UI string contains 'protoLabs Studio' or 'protoLabs Logo'
Bug report links point to homeMaker GitHub repo
App title and branding display 'homeMaker' everywhere

milestones.title: M2: Wire Sensor History DB
milestones.description: Inject SQLite DB into SensorRegistryService so sensor readings persist to the sensor_readings table. Make getHistory() and getHistoryAggregated() return real data.

phases.name: inject-db-into-sensorregistryservice-and-wire-persistence
phases.title: Inject DB into SensorRegistryService and wire persistence
phases.description: Add BetterSqlite3 DB parameter to SensorRegistryService constructor via ServiceContainer. Ensure sensor_readings table is created. Wire report() to persist readings. Verify getHistory() and getHistoryAggregated() query real data.
acceptanceCriteria: SensorRegistryService receives DB via constructor injection
sensor_readings table is created on service init
report() persists readings to SQLite
getHistory() returns real persisted data
getHistoryAggregated() returns real aggregated data

milestones.title: M3: Restore Vendor UI
milestones.description: Rebuild the vendor management frontend that was deleted in PR #42. Backend routes and service are intact.

phases.name: build-vendor-view-components
phases.title: Build vendor view components
phases.description: Rebuild vendor list, vendor card, add-vendor dialog, and vendor detail panel components. Create use-vendors hook for data fetching. Follow existing inventory-view patterns. Use surviving Storybook stories as design spec.
acceptanceCriteria: Vendor list displays all vendors from API
Vendor card shows name, trade, phone, rating
Add-vendor dialog creates new vendors via API
Vendor detail panel shows full info with linked assets
Storybook stories render correctly

phases.name: wire-vendor-route-and-navigation
phases.title: Wire vendor route and navigation
phases.description: Add vendor route file, add nav item in sidebar, verify CRUD operations work end-to-end against existing backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Vendor route is accessible at /vendors
Sidebar shows Vendors nav item
Create, read, update, delete vendors works end-to-end

milestones.title: M4: Home Assistant Integration Client
milestones.description: Build a dedicated HA WebSocket client that connects to Home Assistant, subscribes to entity state changes, and auto-registers entities as homeMaker sensors.

phases.name: build-ha-websocket-client-service
phases.title: Build HA WebSocket client service
phases.description: Create HAClientService that connects to a Home Assistant instance via WebSocket API, authenticates with long-lived access token, subscribes to state_changed events, and auto-registers HA entities as homeMaker sensors via SensorRegistryService.
acceptanceCriteria: HAClientService connects to HA WebSocket API
Authenticates with long-lived access token
Subscribes to state_changed events
Auto-registers HA entities as homeMaker sensors
Reconnects on disconnect with backoff
REST endpoints for connection status and entity list

phases.name: add-ha-configuration-ui
phases.title: Add HA configuration UI
phases.description: Add Home Assistant configuration section in settings with URL, access token, connection status indicator, and entity mapping management.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings UI has Home Assistant section
Can configure HA URL and access token
Shows connection status (connected/disconnected/error)
Lists discovered HA entities with sensor mapping
Can enable/disable individual entity sync

prd.situation: homeMaker has 6 of 8 milestones substantially shipped. Four gaps remain: stale branding, a broken DB wire, a deleted UI, and a missing integration client.
prd.problem: The app still presents as 'protoLabs Studio' in setup/dashboard/sidebar flows. Sensor history writes are dead code. Vendor management has no UI. Home Assistant integration is REST-push only with no proactive connection.
prd.approach: Four milestones, each independently shippable. Rebrand first (user-facing polish), then wire sensor history DB, rebuild vendor UI, and finally add HA client.
prd.results: homeMaker fully branded, all modules functional end-to-end, HA integration bidirectional.
prd.constraints: Single-household deployment only. Tailscale-only networking, no cloud sync. SQLite only, no external databases. No new dependencies unless necessary for HA WebSocket.
